message:
feat(health): database quick_check integration, FTS consistency repair, historical bundle watermark probing, and frankensqlite rule enforcement

Five interconnected improvements to database health verification,
FTS consistency, and historical salvage accuracy:

## AGENTS.md — RULE 2: frankensqlite-only mandate

Add explicit non-negotiable rule prohibiting rusqlite in new code.
All new SQLite access must use frankensqlite (fsqlite). Existing
rusqlite usage is legacy debt to be migrated, not extended. Includes
import pattern guidance for franken_agent_detection connectors.

## Database health probing (storage/sqlite.rs)

New probe_database_health_via_rusqlite() returns a
SqliteDatabaseHealthProbe struct with: quick_check pass/fail, schema
version, FTS schema row count, FTS queryability, message count, and
max message ID. This single function replaces scattered ad-hoc
queries in the doctor and rebuild restart logic.

## FTS consistency repair (storage/sqlite.rs)

New ensure_fts_consistency_via_rusqlite() provides three repair modes:
- AlreadyHealthy: schema current and FTS row count matches messages
- IncrementalCatchUp: FTS rows behind messages, INSERT missing rows
  via LEFT JOIN anti-pattern (no full rebuild needed)
- Rebuilt: schema wrong, FTS unqueryable, or FTS ahead of messages
  (requires full rebuild via rebuild_fts_via_rusqlite)

## Doctor quick_check integration (lib.rs)

The `cass doctor` database check now runs quick_check via the new
health probe before reporting pass/fail. A quick_check failure marks
the database as needing rebuild and skips the FTS sub-check entirely
(FTS status is meaningless if the database itself is corrupt).

## Historical bundle watermark probing (storage/sqlite.rs, indexer/mod.rs)

HistoricalDatabaseBundle now carries a HistoricalBundleProbe with
schema version, FTS state, and max_message_id. The rebuild restart
logic uses best_historical_bundle_message_watermark() to detect when
the canonical database has fallen behind the healthiest historical
bundle — triggering a fresh rebuild from the better source.

should_salvage_historical_databases() now accepts has_pending_historical_bundles
to avoid the expensive discovery scan twice, and correctly skips
salvage when all bundles are already recorded (not just when a seed
was used).

## bookmarks.rs — use Connection::last_insert_rowid()

Replace query_row_map("SELECT last_insert_rowid()") with the direct
Connection::last_insert_rowid() method, avoiding SQL round-trip for
a register read.

## Cargo.lock — fsqlite dependency

Add fsqlite (frankensqlite) to the dependency graph for the Crush
connector migration in franken_agent_detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -45,6 +45,25 @@ If I tell you to do something, even if it goes against what follows below, YOU M
 
 ---
 
+## RULE NUMBER 2: ABSOLUTELY NO RUSQLITE IN NEW CODE — FRANKENSQLITE ONLY
+
+**THIS IS A HARD, NON-NEGOTIABLE RULE. IT HAS BEEN VIOLATED OVER 10 TIMES AND THE OWNER IS DONE TOLERATING IT.**
+
+- **ALL new SQLite code MUST use `frankensqlite` (`fsqlite`).** This applies to CASS, franken_agent_detection, and ANY project in this ecosystem.
+- **NEVER add `rusqlite` as a dependency.** NEVER write `use rusqlite::`. NEVER use `rusqlite::Connection`. Not even "temporarily." Not even "to match the existing pattern."
+- **The existing rusqlite usage is LEGACY DEBT**, not a pattern to follow. Do NOT copy it. Do NOT extend it.
+- **If frankensqlite is missing a feature you need, FIX FRANKENSQLITE.** It lives at `/data/projects/frankensqlite`. Add the missing API there. That is the entire point of the project.
+- **The frankensqlite API** uses `frankensqlite::Connection`, `fsqlite::compat::{ConnectionExt, RowExt, OpenFlags, open_with_flags, ParamValue}`, and `row.get_typed::<T>(idx)` instead of rusqlite's `row.get(idx)`.
+- **For franken_agent_detection connectors**: use `frankensqlite` with the compat layer. Import pattern:
+  ```rust
+  use frankensqlite::Connection as FrankenConnection;
+  use frankensqlite::compat::{ConnectionExt, RowExt, OpenFlags, open_with_flags, param_slice_to_values};
+  ```
+
+**If you are an AI agent reading this and you write `rusqlite` in new code, you are wasting the owner's time and money. This rule exists because agents have repeatedly ignored it. DO NOT BE THE NEXT ONE.**
+
+---
+
 ## Toolchain: Rust & Cargo
 
 We only use **Cargo** in this project, NEVER any other package manager.
